name: La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop
name_original: La Luisiana y Fuentes de Andalucía desde Écija – circular por la Vía Verde de la Campiña
description: Cycle the moderate 38.8-mile La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop, exploring Andalusian plains and historical sites.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Écija traverse a landscape characterized by undulating fields of rain-fed crops, known as La Campiña. The region features the Genil River, the second most important river in Andalusia, which supports various water-based activities and provides natural riverside vegetation. A prominent feature for cyclists is the Vía Verde de la Campiña, an accessible path following an old railway line. Best touring cycling routes around Écija The most popular touring cycling route is La Fuencubierta from Écija – circular via the Vía Verde de la Campiña, a 32.1 miles (51.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an old railway line, offering scenic and accessible paths. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop, a moderate 38.8 miles (62.5 km) path. This route offers views of the Écija countryside and connects to Fuentes de Andalucía. Local touring cyclists also love the The Écija countryside – Fuente Redonda de Las Pinedas loop from Écija, a 33.7 miles (54.2 km) trail leading through the Écija countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Écija is defined by undulating agricultural fields, the Genil River, and the Vía Verde de la Campiña. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Écija are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Écija's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I find around Écija?
A: The region around Écija, known as La Campiña, offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes. You'll find paths traversing undulating fields of rain-fed crops, routes along the Genil River, and accessible trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988122489/la-fuencubierta-from-ecija-circular-via-the-via-verde-de-la-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line. The network includes options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Écija?
A: Yes, Écija offers several easier routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988122489/la-fuencubierta-from-ecija-circular-via-the-via-verde-de-la-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt; is particularly accessible as it follows an old railway line, providing a relatively flat and scenic path. In total, there are 5 easy touring cycling routes available in the area.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes in the Écija area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Écija features 9 difficult routes. These routes often involve more significant elevation changes and cover longer distances across the varied terrain of La Campiña. While specific names are not listed here to avoid redundancy, you can explore the full range of routes on komoot to find one that suits your advanced skill level.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Écija?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Écija are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988122489/la-fuencubierta-from-ecija-circular-via-the-via-verde-de-la-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuencubierta from Écija – circular via the Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988110360/la-campina-greenway-ii-and-fuentes-de-andalucia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Écija countryside – Fuente Redonda de Las Pinedas loop from Écija&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Écija's touring cycling routes offer views of the undulating fields of La Campiña and often run alongside the Genil River, which is the second most important river in Andalusia. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4352235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Valley&lt;/a&gt; with its extensive orange groves, especially beautiful when citrus blossoms are in bloom. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2884260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Écija countryside&lt;/a&gt; itself is a prominent natural highlight.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions are near the cycling routes in Écija?
A: Écija, known as the 'City of Towers,' offers rich historical and cultural attractions. You can explore the central &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6429603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España (El Salón)&lt;/a&gt;, historic churches like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Santa María and Santa Bárbara&lt;/a&gt;, and magnificent palaces such as the Palacio de Peñaflor. The Écija Municipal History Museum, housed in the Palacio de Benamejí, also preserves Roman mosaics and artifacts.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Écija?
A: The Écija region is characterized by its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, with milder weather and beautiful landscapes. Summers can be very hot, while winters are typically mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Écija?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988122489/la-fuencubierta-from-ecija-circular-via-the-via-verde-de-la-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for families. Its accessible nature, following an old railway line, provides a safe and enjoyable experience for cyclists of all ages. Look for routes marked as 'easy' on komoot for other suitable family options.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Écija?
A: The touring cycling routes in Écija are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the scenic beauty of La Campiña, the accessible paths of the Vía Verde, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there options for public transport access to the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific public transport options directly to trailheads are not detailed, Écija itself is a well-connected town. You can often reach the starting points of many routes from the town center, which may be accessible via regional bus services. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for connections to Écija.

Q: Can I find routes that connect Écija with other towns?
A: Yes, some routes connect Écija with other heritage-rich towns in inland Andalusia. For instance, the route to Osuna is part of the Caminos de Pasión network, offering a longer touring experience that links multiple historical locations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988110360/la-campina-greenway-ii-and-fuentes-de-andalucia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop&lt;/a&gt; also connects to Fuentes de Andalucía.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in Écija?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988122489/la-fuencubierta-from-ecija-circular-via-the-via-verde-de-la-campina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuencubierta from Écija – circular via the Vía Verde de la Campiña&lt;/a&gt;, which is 32.1 miles (51.6 km), typically takes around 3 hours and 43 minutes. Longer routes, such as the 38.8 miles (62.5 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988110360/la-campina-greenway-ii-and-fuentes-de-andalucia-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take closer to 5 hours.
tips:
This area is a complex made up of a park and some very well-preserved ruins of Roman baths with medicinal waters.
If you are walking the greenway, it is a good place to stop and rest for a while in the shade.
If you are in Fuentes de Andalucía, take a walk through its alleys to see manor houses - beautiful examples of Andalusian manor architecture from the 18th century.
The beautiful and historic city of Écija with its famous 11 towers, is protected as a historical-artistic complex. If you pass by here, be sure to spend a good and well-deserved visit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows old, reconditioned railway lines, offering a generally flat profile. The surface is predominantly compacted earth, though some sections are asphalt. Be prepared for some irregular or rocky stretches, especially when leaving the flat river valley, and some areas may have speed bumps. While suitable for cycling, some parts might be challenging for road bikes due to the mixed surface, making mountain bikes a better choice.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde de la Campiña, which forms part of this loop, is generally considered suitable for cycling, but some sources suggest it's 'suitable with difficulties' due to an irregular surface and speed bumps. Komoot classifies similar routes in the area as 'moderate,' requiring good fitness. While not overly strenuous in terms of elevation, the mixed terrain might be challenging for absolute beginners on certain bike types.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers a blend of natural landscapes and cultural immersion. You'll traverse fertile plains with extensive fields of sunflowers, cereal crops, and olive groves. Key points of interest include the Roman Baths of La Luisiana, the town of Fuentes de Andalucía, and the historic city of Écija. The trail also incorporates six bridges and the Las Tablas tunnel.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The landscape is particularly vibrant and colorful in spring, making it an ideal time to visit. The extensive fields of sunflowers, cereal crops, and olive groves are at their most beautiful then. Always check weather conditions before your trip, especially for extreme heat in summer or heavy rains in winter.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get water along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fuentes de Andalucía is noted as a convenient stop for replenishing food and water when cycling the greenway. Other towns along the broader Vía Verde de la Campiña, such as Écija and La Luisiana, also offer amenities.

faqs.question: Is the La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde de la Campiña is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs can vary. It's usually permitted to bring dogs on a leash, especially on greenways. However, always ensure your dog is under control, and be mindful of other cyclists and agricultural areas. Carry water for your dog, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle the Vía Verde de la Campiña or the connecting roads that form this loop. Greenways in Spain are generally free and open to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Campiña Greenway II and Fuentes de Andalucía Loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the towns along the route. Fuentes de Andalucía is a key point along the greenway and a good starting or ending point for a loop, so look for public parking options within the town to access the trail.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mixed terrain and potential for irregular surfaces, we recommend a touring bicycle or mountain bike. Pack essentials like a repair kit, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is always advised. If cycling in spring, consider layers as temperatures can change.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop incorporates sections of several official trails. It primarily follows the Vía Verde de la Campiña II (Écija - Marchena), and also includes parts of the Camino Natural Vía Verde de la Campiña II, and the broader Vía Verde de la Campiña. You may also encounter sections of the Cordel de la Luisana.

faqs.question: Are there any current closures or important warnings for the greenway?
faqs.answer: Yes, as of February 2026, due to heavy rain and stormy weather, a significant portion of the Vía Verde de la Campiña is impassable. It is currently only safe to travel between Fuentes de Andalucía and the wooden bridge. Cyclists are strongly advised to exercise extreme caution and verify the current conditions of the greenway before planning their trip.
